summary: Restore is now source-backed by a dry-run endpoint/job with permission gates, audit metadata, and focused tests.
checks: backup.restore uses backup write policy with medium risk for dry-run and high risk for live restore.
BackupRestoreService creates restore dry-run evidence from completed backup runs.
dryRun:false restore requests are recorded and blocked until approval, target isolation, and rollback validation exist.
Restore audit metadata includes source backup id, validation query, rollback plan, and resulting status.
New source files remain under the 200-line ceiling.
Board JSON parses and git diff whitespace hygiene passes.
remaining_risks: S020 clears the first-class restore implementation blocker, but production restore still needs an approved staging/prod rehearsal before release signoff.
Live provider/resource provisioning and production backup/rollback rehearsals still require approved credentials and targets.
